insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel.
insight.developer_impact: Developers using the Storefront API should update their queries to include the channelId argument when necessary. This change enables more flexibility in querying data specific to different sales channels. Developers should review their existing queries and update them to take advantage of this new feature. The Storefront API documentation should be consulted for more information on using the @inContext directive with the channelId argument.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more accurate and sales channel-specific data being displayed in their storefronts, enhancing their overall shopping experience.
insight.why_it_matters: This update matters because it allows for more precise control over the data fetched from the Storefront API, enabling developers to build more tailored and sales channel-specific experiences for merchants and their customers.

also_worth_knowing.text: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, allowing early access to new inventory APIs.
CLI version 4.5.2 fixes a warning when running theme commands and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: Hydrogen CLI version 13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts during upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: App Store requirement 4.1.2 mandates unique app names to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: Storefront API version 2026-10 supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 includes discount allocations for bundle components.
CLI version 4.5.0 introduces new store commands, including listing and store creation.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1
updates.summary: Hydrogen CLI version 13.0.1 reads disabled command metadata and preserves actionable build errors during deployment.

updates.title: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38
updates.summary: Create Hydrogen version 5.0.38 adds an explicit app alias to new Hydrogen projects and uses jsconfig.json for JavaScript projects.

recap.headline: Shopify updates focus on GraphQL Admin API, Hydrogen, and Storefront API enhancements

themes.title: GraphQL Admin API updates
themes.detail: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, and the card-deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate. Additionally, new fields have been added to the ExchangeLineItem type.

themes.title: Hydrogen and CLI updates
themes.detail: Hydrogen has been updated to version 13.0.2 with new flags for non-interactive shop and storefront creation, and the Shopify CLI has been updated to version 4.5.2 with bug fixes and improvements.

themes.title: Storefront API and App Store updates
themes.detail: The Storefront API now supports the channelId argument in the @inContext directive, and the App Store has updated requirements for app names to reduce merchant confusion. POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components.

actions.text: Enable physical inventory feature preview on a development store
Update apps to use Shopify-issued mTLS client certificates for card-deposit endpoint
Review and update app names to comply with App Store requirements
